title: Malaxis intermedia
intro: Malaxis intermedia, also known as the lesser yellow adder's mouth, is a species of orchid found in various parts of the world, including Asia and the Americas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis intermedia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Malaxis intermedia is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, or as a tea, which is said to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
taxonomy: Malaxis intermedia is a species within the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It is further classified as a member of the genus Malaxis, which is a subgenus within the genus Dendrobium. Malaxis intermedia is a terrestrial orchid native to China, specifically in the Sichuan, Yunnan, and Guizhou provinces. The classification of Malaxis intermedia is also sometimes placed within the subfamily Orchidoideae, which is part of the family Orchidaceae. The classification may vary depending on the source and the current taxonomic understanding of the species.
